skill.name: nextjs-optimization
skill.description: Optimize Next.js 15 applications for performance, Core Web Vitals, caching, bundle size, images, fonts, SEO, and production configuration.
skill.summary: Improve Next.js 15 performance with App Router, Core Web Vitals, caching, bundle, image, and production checks.
skill.author: AI-Vibe-Prompts

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, environment, and dynamic import patterns, but review found them in Markdown guidance and Next.js examples. No prompt injection, credential exfiltration, malicious code execution, or unsafe secret handling was found in SKILL.md. The skill is low risk because it may guide an assistant to run normal project inspection and build tools.

low_findings.title: Documentation-Only Shell Command Examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown examples for project inspection, build analysis, Lighthouse checks, and dependency detection. They are not hidden execution logic, and the skill file contains no runnable script wrapper that executes them automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged commands are inside fenced Markdown examples and match the skill purpose of auditing a local Next.js project. I found no evidence that SKILL.md contains executable automation that runs shell commands without user context.

low_findings.title: Legitimate Next.js Dynamic Import and Fetch Examples
low_findings.description: The dynamic import and fetch findings are normal Next.js optimization examples for bundle splitting and cache behavior. The URLs are placeholders or localhost examples, and no credential or user data is sent to an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code shows instructional examples using next/dynamic, example.com, localhost, and analytics.example.com. I found no evidence of data exfiltration or obfuscated network behavior.

low_findings.title: Environment and Browser Storage References Are Benign Examples
low_findings.description: The environment access finding is a production configuration example using process.env.NODE_ENV. The browser storage finding is a textual note that client components may use browser APIs such as localStorage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable example reads only NODE_ENV for removeConsole behavior, and the localStorage reference is descriptive text. I found no evidence of secret file reads, credential collection, or browser storage exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Text Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to be scanner matches inside ordinary prose or metadata examples, such as description fields. SKILL.md does not recommend MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows Next.js optimization language and SEO metadata examples, not cryptographic implementation guidance. I found no evidence of weak crypto usage in the skill.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Signals Are Project Context Checks
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are tied to normal framework detection and documentation text. The skill instructs inspection of local project files to determine Next.js usage and optimization opportunities.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant context is framework and performance analysis, not host or network reconnaissance. I found no evidence of commands intended to enumerate the user system beyond normal project review.

content.value_statement: Next.js applications can lose speed through large bundles, poor image handling, weak caching, and unnecessary client rendering. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused performance checks and practical App Router improvements.
actual_capabilities: Reviews Next.js version, routing style, and production readiness indicators.
Identifies opportunities to use Server Components, Suspense, streaming, and dynamic imports.
Guides image, font, metadata, and cache improvements for App Router projects.
Suggests bundle analysis and Lighthouse checks for Core Web Vitals validation.
Provides a structured optimization report with issues, applied fixes, estimated impact, and next steps.
limitations: It is written for Next.js 15 and App Router patterns, so older projects may need adaptation.
Performance impact estimates require verification with real Lighthouse or Web Vitals data.
It does not replace manual review of business logic, accessibility, or production infrastructure.
Some recommendations require project-specific testing before deployment.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Product Launch
use_cases.description: Audit a Next.js application before release and prioritize fixes that affect load time and Core Web Vitals.

use_cases.title: Reduce Frontend Bundle Size
use_cases.description: Find large client components, unnecessary client boundaries, and imports that should be split or narrowed.

use_cases.title: Improve Production Readiness
use_cases.description: Review image handling, font loading, caching, metadata, and next.config.js settings for a production site.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Next.js Performance Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a project before optimization work begins.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Next.js project for common performance issues. Focus on images, fonts, routing style, metadata, and Core Web Vitals risks.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize App Router Rendering
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of Server Components, Client Components, and data loading.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my App Router pages and components. Recommend where to use Server Components, Suspense, streaming, caching, or dynamic rendering.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Bundle Reduction Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced bundle analysis for a slow production application.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find likely causes of a large client bundle in this Next.js project. Prioritize dynamic imports, import cleanup, and client component reductions.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Production Optimization Report
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced audit with implementation guidance and verification steps.
prompt_templates.prompt: Produce a production optimization report for this Next.js 15 app. Include critical issues, recommended changes, expected performance impact, and validation steps.

output_examples.input: Review a Next.js homepage with slow Largest Contentful Paint.
output: Identifies the likely hero image as the main LCP candidate.
Recommends priority image loading, fixed dimensions, modern formats, and server-rendered above-the-fold content.
Suggests validating the change with Lighthouse and real Web Vitals data.

output_examples.input: Analyze a dashboard with a large JavaScript bundle.
output: Flags heavy interactive components and broad imports as likely bundle contributors.
Recommends moving static sections to Server Components and splitting non-critical widgets.
Provides a prioritized plan for measuring bundle size after each change.

output_examples.input: Prepare a Next.js app for production deployment.
output: Reviews production config, cache settings, image formats, metadata, and font loading.
Separates critical launch blockers from lower-priority improvements.
Lists verification steps for build output, Lighthouse results, and regression checks.

best_practices: Measure current performance before changing implementation details.
Prefer Server Components unless a component needs browser APIs or interactive state.
Validate each optimization with builds, Lighthouse, and real user metrics when available.
anti_patterns: Adding use client at the top of large page trees without a clear interaction need.
Using raw image tags or remote images without dimensions and format planning.
Making many performance changes at once without measuring their effect.

faq.question: Does this skill modify code automatically?
faq.answer: It can guide code edits when used with an assistant that has edit tools. Review all changes before deployment.

faq.question: Which Next.js version does it target?
faq.answer: It targets Next.js 15 and App Router patterns. Some guidance also applies to Next.js 13 and 14.

faq.question: Can it improve Core Web Vitals?
faq.answer: It identifies common causes of poor LCP, FID, and CLS scores and suggests practical fixes.

faq.question: Does it need internet access?
faq.answer: The skill itself is documentation. Some suggested validation tools may inspect local or localhost pages.

faq.question: Is it safe for private repositories?
faq.answer: The reviewed skill does not include credential exfiltration logic. Still review assistant actions before running commands.

faq.question: Can it replace a full performance audit?
faq.answer: No. It helps with common Next.js optimization work, but production results need measurement and expert review.
